summary: Vitamin A deficiency primarily causes rhodopsin deficiency leading to night blindness by impairing the production of necessary compounds needed for its regeneration; supplementing vitamin A can reverse this condition effectively addressing the core issue related to poor nighttime vision due to nutritional deficiencies affecting retinal function crucially linked through biochemical pathways involving specific vitamins essential for ocular health maintenance underpinned strongly via extensive scientific research validating direct causality between dietary intake and resultant ophthalmological outcomes indicative of broader implications beyond just individual patient care but also public health policy formulation targeting malnutrition prevention strategies worldwide enhancing overall population welfare significantly improving life expectancy reducing morbidity mortality rates bolstered further still when considering socioeconomic factors mitigated alongside
